currentFocus: Automation shutdown closeout. The high-throughput standing loop, Ghidra/RE sentinel, storage/Ghidra sentinel, validation/drift sentinel, and chief heartbeat have been stopped by user request. Main and origin/main are aligned at the pushed texture/resource/decode static-routing ledger commit, and the only source change in this closeout is a narrow Ghidra legacy backup-root provenance guard plus concise state/posture cleanup. Future work should run one bounded slice at a time from the main coordinator thread with explicit ownership, consults, gates, and stop conditions.

integrationWave.preStateUpdateRefProof: HEAD, origin/main, and live remote refs/heads/main matched a9fe757e238a237eac8aa8f7f4e12d1bad57ec60 with divergence 0 0 before this state-only baton update.

automationPosture.summary: The previous closeout recorded deleted standing-loop automations; they were temporarily restored by the 2026-07-02 user request, then intensified on 2026-07-03. The user then stopped the automation experiment on 2026-07-03 because hygiene and ownership overhead outweighed the scheduling value. The standing-wave launcher, Ghidra/RE sentinel, storage/Ghidra sentinel, validation/drift sentinel, and chief heartbeat are not current operating loops. Re-enable only from an explicit new bounded automation plan.

patchBench.summary: PatchBench receipt rendering is wired through PatchBenchSafeCopyReceiptText using a BinaryPatchesPage-owned mapper from the AppCore safe-copy receipt DTO. Group scan summaries remain sourced from visible rows/tracks.
limits: BinaryPatchesPage and AppCore still own live receipt construction, I/O, launch planning, process control, catalog semantics, Host/Join boundary injection/fallback, online boundaries, and runtime-proof boundaries.
PatchBenchSafeCopyReceiptText remains primitive-state formatting plus fail-closed display validation only.

lore.summary: Lore ZIP/package probe, builder checker, and AppCore tests cover above-root and encoded above-root packed links, AppCore-compatible document-id grammar, case-variant content-id lookup, exact relativePath equality, byteLength/hash checks, non-object JSONL rows, and generic public-safe failures.

rebuildAndOriginalMapping.summary: TMM ARM4 readiness-gate continuity now has a short npm wrapper, test:tmm-arm4-readiness-gate-proof-plan, and the readiness-gate checker validates that wrapper. The original-system crosswalk and source-only internal tooling vocabulary map were added, the internal viewer/editor command-boundary proof plan/checker keeps -modelviewer and -cutsceneeditor source-only and blocked from product launch surfaces, the AYA resource tag-family static contract/checker keeps LVLR, WRES, ERES, LNDS, PAGE, GDIE, MESH, and TEXT as loader-contract vocabulary pending later higher-authority proof, the render-resource bridge static contract/checker routes MESH/TEXT only into existing renderer/material/mesh/texture static docs, and the career progression static bridge contract/checker routes CCareer/level_structure/CGame/CEndLevelData only into save/career static docs, and the texture/resource/decode static routing ledger/checker records sanitized lane ids, anchor function names, historical Wave1163/Wave1216 provenance-only status, latest backup-class redaction, static-accounting authority, false/zero guard summaries, and duplicate-lane redaction reconciliation.

The AYA tag-family static-contract checker is a source/static planning guard; it is not corpus proof, runtime parser proof, extractor execution, importer execution, generated asset output, renderer proof, AppCore/WinUI/CLI/release support, or rebuild parity evidence.

The career progression static bridge-contract checker is a source/static planning guard; it is not runtime save/load proof, mission-outcome persistence proof, objective UI proof, Goodies behavior proof, live MissionScript command-effect proof, patch proof, gameplay proof, generated save/asset output, AppCore/WinUI/CLI/Godot/release support, product exposure, or rebuild parity evidence.

No command arming, importer execution, private asset read, generated payload output, runtime proof, rebuild parity, visual/gameplay parity, or no-noticeable-difference proof.

latestStorageSentinel.summary: User-authorized cleanup codified the future backup/proof posture to a maintainer-local ignored removable-root policy, created a fresh live-project Ghidra backup with file-count/byte/hash verification and read-only headless read-back, removed legacy external Ghidra backup/source archive/runtime proof archive roots, and cleared superseded removable-root pre-cleanup scratch. Raw runtime proof bundles are no longer treated as permanent evidence; tracked proof summaries, validators, ledgers, recreation instructions, and future temporary proof outputs are the durable posture.
latestStorageSentinel.retainedEvidence: Latest verified live-project Ghidra backup id BEA_20260703-152228Z_live_project_backup_verified is retained in ignored local storage with local cleanup/backup manifests. Tracked docs retain public-safe proof conclusions and boundaries; raw historical runtime proof bundles and old source-scratch worktree copies were deleted per user direction.
latestStorageSentinel.inventorySummary: Legacy external Ghidra backup root, source-scratch archive root, and runtime proof archive root were removed. Removable scratch now retains only the verified live-project Ghidra backup plus local cleanup manifests/policy. External archive drive free space measured about 928.14 GiB after cleanup; removable scratch free space measured about 119.07 GiB.
blockers: Raw historical runtime proof bundles are intentionally deleted; future audits must rely on tracked public-safe summaries/validators or regenerate bounded temporary proof output.
Fresh future destructive cleanup still requires candidate-specific local manifests and retained replacement/summary decisions.
Do not treat removable scratch storage as the only durable copy of current source, release assets, or active claim-bearing evidence.
